+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: aa80
+title: Group dropdown is empty — runtime FieldDefs are missing groupable flag
+---
+## What
+
+After the Group migration (commit pending — task `01KRE1ZTYJ5PPTQ29K72KE88B5`), the Group By popover on a real perspective shows an empty dropdown. The new `denormalize_perspective_fields` filter (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dynamic_sources.rs`) only emits fields where `FieldDef.groupable == Some(true)`. Tests pass because the test fixtures hand-construct FieldDefs with `groupable: Some(true)`. Production data does not match: the seed/default FieldDefs are constructed with `groupable: None`, so the filter drops everything.
+
+## Confirmed evidence
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/assignees.yaml:14` → `groupable: true`
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/color.yaml:10` → `groupable: true`
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/tags.yaml:20` → `groupable: true`
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs:976,1013,1052,1186,1511,1668` → `groupable: None` (six seed-construction sites)
+- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/context.rs:472,939` → `groupable: None`
+- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/compute.rs:195,214` → `groupable: None`
+
+The YAML says these fields are groupable; the in-memory representation says they are not. The user's perspective fields resolve through the in-memory representation, so the filter drops them.
+
+## Investigation steps (do these before changing anything)
+
+1. **Trace one user-visible field end-to-end.** Pick `status` or `assignees`. From `FieldsContext` load to `denormalize_perspective_fields` lookup: where does its `FieldDef.groupable` come from? Is the YAML's `groupable: true` actually parsed and stored, or is `defaults.rs` overriding/shadowing it?
+2. **Identify the canonical FieldDef source.** Whichever of `defaults.rs` / `compute.rs` / `context.rs` is the path the live app uses must carry `groupable: Some(true)` for groupable fields. Fix THAT path.
+3. **Confirm test/runtime parity.** The end-to-end test `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/options_enrichment.rs` used `assignees` (groupable) + `title` (non-groupable). That test passed, which means the FieldsContext path it took DID see `groupable: Some(true)` for `assignees`. Why doesn't the live app see the same? Either the test fixture loads from YAML and live data doesn't, or vice versa.
+
+## Files likely to change
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` (six `groupable: None` sites — at least the user-grouping fields like `status`, `assignees`, `tags`, `color`, `priority` need `Some(true)`)
+- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/context.rs:472,939` (if these are on the live load path)
+- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/compute.rs:195,214` (computed fields — should they ever be groupable? probably no, but verify)
+
+The fix may also be entirely in **loader code** — if YAML files DO carry `groupable: true` but a deserialization helper or merge function drops it during runtime construction, fix the loader instead of the seed constants.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] On a real board with the default perspective loaded, opening the Group By popover shows at least the obviously-groupable fields (`status`, `assignees`, `tags`, `color`, `priority` — whichever apply per the entity schema). The dropdown is NOT empty.
+- [ ] Fields that are correctly non-groupable (e.g. `title`, `description`, `id`) stay absent from the dropdown.
+- [ ] Existing test `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` still passes (no regression on the contract).
+- [ ] New end-to-end test pins the production fixture path: load FieldsContext via the same code path the live app uses, build a perspective referencing groupable fields, assert the resolver returns them.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] **New regression test** in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/options_enrichment.rs` (or a sibling file): `perspective_group_command_emits_groupable_fields_from_live_field_loader` — uses the live loader path (whatever `kanban-app::list_commands_for_scope` uses, NOT a hand-constructed `FieldsContext`), references at least `status` and `assignees`, asserts both appear in the emitted `perspective.group` `options`. This test would FAIL before the fix and PASS after.
+- [ ] **Unit test** in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` (or `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/context.rs`, wherever the canonical FieldDef source lives): assert that the default `status` field def has `groupable: Some(true)`. Likewise for any other field the user should be able to group by.
+- [ ] **Smoke** existing test `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` still passes — no regression on the non-groupable filter direction.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the new regression test FIRST (it should fail, demonstrating the empty-dropdown bug). Then trace the FieldDef path and fix the root cause. Then watch the test pass.
+- **Do NOT loosen the filter** in `denormalize_perspective_fields` to "allow None" or similar. The strict `== Some(true)` check is correct; the bug is upstream. Fixing it the wrong way (treat `None` as `true`) would silently let non-groupable fields through and re-introduce the original regression the Group migration's review caught.
+- **Profile-first if needed**: if it's not obvious from a code read which path the live app uses, add a one-shot `tracing::info!` in `denormalize_perspective_fields` logging each field id + `groupable` value, run the app once, see what's in the log, then revert the trace and fix the source. #command-driven-ui #bug
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Group dropdown is empty — runtime FieldDefs are missing groupable flag

## What

After the Group migration (commit pending — task `01KRE1ZTYJ5PPTQ29K72KE88B5`), the Group By popover on a real perspective shows an empty dropdown. The new `denormalize_perspective_fields` filter (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dynamic_sources.rs`) only emits fields where `FieldDef.groupable == Some(true)`. Tests pass because the test fixtures hand-construct FieldDefs with `groupable: Some(true)`. Production data does not match: the seed/default FieldDefs are constructed with `groupable: None`, so the filter drops everything.

## Confirmed evidence

- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/assignees.yaml:14` → `groupable: true`
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/color.yaml:10` → `groupable: true`
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/definitions/tags.yaml:20` → `groupable: true`
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs:976,1013,1052,1186,1511,1668` → `groupable: None` (six seed-construction sites)
- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/context.rs:472,939` → `groupable: None`
- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/compute.rs:195,214` → `groupable: None`

The YAML says these fields are groupable; the in-memory representation says they are not. The user's perspective fields resolve through the in-memory representation, so the filter drops them.

## Investigation steps (do these before changing anything)

1. **Trace one user-visible field end-to-end.** Pick `status` or `assignees`. From `FieldsContext` load to `denormalize_perspective_fields` lookup: where does its `FieldDef.groupable` come from? Is the YAML's `groupable: true` actually parsed and stored, or is `defaults.rs` overriding/shadowing it?
2. **Identify the canonical FieldDef source.** Whichever of `defaults.rs` / `compute.rs` / `context.rs` is the path the live app uses must carry `groupable: Some(true)` for groupable fields. Fix THAT path.
3. **Confirm test/runtime parity.** The end-to-end test `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/options_enrichment.rs` used `assignees` (groupable) + `title` (non-groupable). That test passed, which means the FieldsContext path it took DID see `groupable: Some(true)` for `assignees`. Why doesn't the live app see the same? Either the test fixture loads from YAML and live data doesn't, or vice versa.

## Files likely to change

- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` (six `groupable: None` sites — at least the user-grouping fields like `status`, `assignees`, `tags`, `color`, `priority` need `Some(true)`)
- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/context.rs:472,939` (if these are on the live load path)
- `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/compute.rs:195,214` (computed fields — should they ever be groupable? probably no, but verify)

The fix may also be entirely in **loader code** — if YAML files DO carry `groupable: true` but a deserialization helper or merge function drops it during runtime construction, fix the loader instead of the seed constants.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] On a real board with the default perspective loaded, opening the Group By popover shows at least the obviously-groupable fields (`status`, `assignees`, `tags`, `color`, `priority` — whichever apply per the entity schema). The dropdown is NOT empty.
- [ ] Fields that are correctly non-groupable (e.g. `title`, `description`, `id`) stay absent from the dropdown.
- [ ] Existing test `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` still passes (no regression on the contract).
- [ ] New end-to-end test pins the production fixture path: load FieldsContext via the same code path the live app uses, build a perspective referencing groupable fields, assert the resolver returns them.

## Tests

- [ ] **New regression test** in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/options_enrichment.rs` (or a sibling file): `perspective_group_command_emits_groupable_fields_from_live_field_loader` — uses the live loader path (whatever `kanban-app::list_commands_for_scope` uses, NOT a hand-constructed `FieldsContext`), references at least `status` and `assignees`, asserts both appear in the emitted `perspective.group` `options`. This test would FAIL before the fix and PASS after.
- [ ] **Unit test** in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` (or `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/context.rs`, wherever the canonical FieldDef source lives): assert that the default `status` field def has `groupable: Some(true)`. Likewise for any other field the user should be able to group by.
- [ ] **Smoke** existing test `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` still passes — no regression on the non-groupable filter direction.
- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — green.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the new regression test FIRST (it should fail, demonstrating the empty-dropdown bug). Then trace the FieldDef path and fix the root cause. Then watch the test pass.
- **Do NOT loosen the filter** in `denormalize_perspective_fields` to "allow None" or similar. The strict `== Some(true)` check is correct; the bug is upstream. Fixing it the wrong way (treat `None` as `true`) would silently let non-groupable fields through and re-introduce the original regression the Group migration's review caught.
- **Profile-first if needed**: if it's not obvious from a code read which path the live app uses, add a one-shot `tracing::info!` in `denormalize_perspective_fields` logging each field id + `groupable` value, run the app once, see what's in the log, then revert the trace and fix the source. #command-driven-ui #bug

+The original hypothesis was wrong — but the symptom was real. Documenting the actual root cause so future readers don't re-derive it:
+
+**Hypothesis was wrong because**: All six `groupable: None` sites in `defaults.rs`, both sites in `compute.rs`, and both sites in `context.rs` are inside `#[cfg(test)]` blocks (`make_test_field`, `make_text_field`, computed-field test fixtures). They are **not** in the production load path. The production path is `KanbanContext::open` → `build_entity_context` → `FieldsContext::from_yaml_sources` → `serde_yaml_ng::from_str::<FieldDef>(yaml)`. With `#[serde(default)]` on `groupable: Option<bool>`, the deserializer correctly maps a missing field to `None` and `groupable: true` to `Some(true)`. The unit test `groupable_deserializes_when_present` in `swissarmyhammer-fields/src/types.rs` pins this; the existing end-to-end `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` test was already passing precisely because the live YAML loader correctly carried `groupable: Some(true)` for `assignees`.
+
+**Actual root cause**: `denormalize_perspective_fields` sourced the Group By picker's options from `perspective.fields[]` — the perspective's *visible column list*. +
+The legacy `<GroupSelector>` (pre-migration) sourced its `fields` prop from `getSchema(entityType)?.fields ?? []` — the **entity schema**, not the perspective's column list. The command-driven-ui migration moved the filter to the backend but accidentally swapped the source. The strict `== Some(true)` filter was correct; the upstream source was wrong.
+
+**Fix** (in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dynamic_sources.rs`):
+
+1. New helper `entity_type_for_perspective(perspective, views)` resolves the perspective's bound entity_type via its `view_id` (strict path) or `view` kind (legacy fallback, ambiguity-safe).
+2. `denormalize_perspective_fields` now enumerates `fields_ctx.fields_for_entity(entity_type)` and filters by `groupable == Some(true)`. The function signature gained a `views: &[ViewDef]` parameter.
+3. `gather_perspectives` holds the views lock for the iteration and threads the slice into `denormalize_perspective_fields`.
+
+The filter remains strict (`== Some(true)`) — no regression on the non-groupable contract. The existing test `perspective_group_command_drops_non_groupable_fields_end_to_end` still passes because `assignees` is groupable on the `task` schema and `title` is not, regardless of source.
+
+**Surface of change**: only `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dynamic_sources.rs` and its accompanying tests in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/options_enrichment.rs`. Six existing unit tests in `dynamic_sources::tests` were rewritten to test the new "source from entity schema" contract; six new tests cover both happy/negative cases and the view_id strict/legacy resolution paths.

+The previous fix established that:
